title: Chicken & Rice with Peanut Curry Sauce
summary: A rich and flavourful chicken and rice dish with a creamy peanut curry sauce. Balanced with fresh lime, crispy onions, and cavolo nero, this meal is perfect for prepping ahead.

method.description: In a bowl, mix the chicken thighs with half the garlic (peeled and bashed) and the kecap manis. Season with salt and black pepper, then set aside to marinate.

Heat a drizzle of oil in a frying pan with high sides over medium heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken and fry for 10-12 mins, turning occasionally, until cooked through and slightly charred. If your sauce is reducing too much turn the heat down to low. Transfer to a plate to rest.
Strip the cavolo nero leaves from their stems.
In the same pan, add the cavolo nero leaves and cook until wilted. Transfer to the chicken plate.
Add the remaining bashed garlic, red Thai curry paste, tomato purée, and peanut butter to the empty pan. Cook, stirring, for 2-3 mins until the mixture darkens in colour.
Pour in the coconut milk and cook, stirring, until the sauce thickens. Remove from heat and season with salt, lime juice, and a touch of sugar if needed.
Reheat the rice and divide it into serving boxes. Slice the chicken thighs and place them on top with the cavolo nero. Pour over the peanut curry sauce.
Garnish with crispy onions and fresh coriander. Box up and refrigerate until needed. Reheat before serving.

tips: Storage: Keep refrigerated in airtight containers for up to 3 days. Reheat before serving.
Freezing: Store portions in the freezer for up to 1 month. Defrost overnight in the fridge before reheating.
Substitutions: Swap kecap manis oyster sauce for a less sweet profile. Use spinach instead of cavolo nero if preferred.
Serving Ideas: Add a side of pickled vegetables or a fresh cucumber salad for extra crunch and freshness.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
